notes: 6 closed 5-vertex LWPOLYLINEs on floor-plan INSUL (insulation) layer; anonymous plan fragment tracing envelope insulation along exterior walls.

rationale.evidence: All 6 entities are LWPOLYLINE (dxf histogram LWPOLYLINE=6), each with vertices=5 (a closed quadrilateral), on the single xref layer X-평면도(기본형)$0$INSUL (handles 483F-4844). INSUL denotes 단열재/thermal insulation and the layer's parent 평면도(기본형) is a standard-type floor plan, so this anonymous block is insulation graphics of the plan. No LINE, INSERT, TEXT, HATCH, or dimension entities are present.
rationale.rule: A block composed solely of closed LWPOLYLINEs on a floor-plan INSUL (insulation) layer is a plan-geometry fragment that traces the insulated building envelope (exterior/party walls); classify it as 평면 부분도 and treat insulation as a wall-location indicator, not a container of wall-line entities.
rationale.counterfactual: If these polylines resolved to long parallel collinear edges at ~100-200mm wall spacing, or if the layer were 벽체/WALL instead of INSUL, wall_likelihood would rise and specific wall-face handles could be listed; if the layer named furniture, a symbol, or text, the role would shift to 가구/심볼/기타.
rationale.uncertainty: The projection gives only vertices=5 with no vertex coordinates, so I cannot confirm whether each polygon is an insulation batt or a wall-poché fill, cannot extract any wall-face line, and cannot compute a bbox. The 0.4 wall_likelihood reflects wall-association (insulation co-locates with exterior walls), not verified wall-line content; wall_line_handles is [] because every entity is a closed insulation polygon, not a linear wall face.

wall_line_handles.reason: long horizontal edge; 50mm paired gap = bracket flange not wall
parallel horizontal 50mm from 4858 — too thin for a wall

notes: Flagpole-holder (국기계양대) fixture symbol: 2 WIPEOUTs + nested fixture INSERT + 1120×126mm bracket of 20 LINEs on layer 0.

rationale.evidence: Mixed histogram INSERT=1, LINE=20, WIPEOUT=2. The INSERT places block X-평면도(기본형)$0$국기계양대 (국기계양대 = flagpole holder) on its own layer; the 20 LINEs and 2 WIPEOUTs sit on layer 0. bbox [-560,-103.5,0,560,22.5,0] ≈ 1120×126mm — fixture-scale, not room-scale. Lines 4858/485A form a 1076×50mm inner bar and the remaining LINEs a notched outer frame; the WIPEOUTs mask the background so the symbol reads cleanly.
rationale.rule: A block combining WIPEOUT masks, a nested named-fixture INSERT, and a small (~1m) closed LINE frame on layer 0 is a plan fixture/symbol (심볼): it is inserted at a wall or parapet but does not itself contain structural wall geometry.
rationale.counterfactual: If the LINEs spanned meters as long parallel pairs at ~100-200mm spacing with no nested fixture INSERT and no WIPEOUT, the block would read as wall geometry (평면 부분도) with high wall_likelihood; removing the 국기계양대 INSERT alone would drop the symbol reading.
rationale.uncertainty: I cannot inspect the nested 국기계양대 block's contents, so wall lines embedded there cannot be ruled out; the 50mm parallel gap of the inner-bar lines could be misread as a thin wall, but at ~1m fixture scale it is a bracket flange, not a partition.

The 0.4 wall_likelihood reflects wall-association (insulation co-locates with exterior walls), not verified wall-line content; wall_line_handles is [] because every entity is a closed insulation polygon, not a linear wall face.
